macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indices Close Lower on March 19 with Modest Declines
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.27% to 6606.49, Nasdaq 100 down 0.29% to 24355.275, and Dow Jones down 0.44% to around 46,021, amid lighter trading volume and a VIX spike indicating heightened investor anxiety. Russell 2000 outperformed, rising 0.65% to 2494.711, while energy sector led gains up 1.59%.[1][2]

items.title: VIX Fear Gauge Spikes to 25.09 Amid Market Anxiety
items.summary: The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) rose over 12% to 25.09 before closing down 4.07% at 24.07, signaling increased demand for hedging despite modest index declines. This reflected weak conviction in the sell-off with below-average volume.[1][2]

items.title: Sector Rotation Favors Tech and Energy Over Utilities
items.summary: Technology sector (XLK) gained 0.33% and energy (XLE) rose 1.59%, while utilities (XLU) fell 0.41%; regional banks (KRE, KBE) up at least 0.68%. Gold stocks underperformed, with AngloGold Ashanti down over 7%.[1][2]

items.title: Tesla Leads Mag 7 Declines, Down Over 3%
items.summary: Tesla dropped more than 3%, heading declines among the Magnificent 7 stocks, contributing to broader tech caution despite sector gains. All major indices closed below their 200-day moving averages.[2]

items.title: Global Central Banks Maintain Hawkish Stance
items.summary: Global central banks held a hawkish posture, influencing market sentiment amid US index declines and volatility spike. No specific Fed announcements detailed, but contributed to cautious trading.[2]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Loses Key Support Level Amid Sell-Off Fears
items.summary: Bitcoin has broken a critical support level, potentially heading toward $80,000 or deeper lows below recent bottoms, with analysts warning of a breaking point and possible bullish divergence at major levels. Market discussions highlight Fed rates and Bitcoin sell-off pressures.
items.source: YouTube - Bitcoin at a Breaking Point & NFA LIVE

items.title: SEC Clarifies Major Cryptos Like Bitcoin Are Not Securities
items.summary: The US SEC has issued guidelines stating most major cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP, are not securities but digital commodities, reducing regulatory uncertainty and boosting institutional adoption expectations.
items.source: YouTube - Bitcoin Is No Longer a Stock

items.title: XRP Faces Alleged Price Suppression Amid Institutional Accumulation
items.summary: Evidence suggests coordinated suppression of XRP price via a '9:30 a.m. kill switch' targeting retail, while 7 billion XRP exits exchanges and institutional players like Deutsche Bank accumulate ahead of Clarity Act and Mbridge project expansion.

items.title: Crypto Market Experiences Mega Crash Linked to Broader Sell-Off
items.summary: A significant crypto market crash is reported alongside gold, with most coins negative in the last day; discussions point to manipulative moves, support levels, and potential pumps amid global uncertainties.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026: Physical AI Ecosystem Expansion with Industrial Partners
items.summary: NVIDIA announced expanded partnerships with industrial software giants ABB, Universal Robots, and KUKA to integrate physical AI models and simulation tools for manufacturing robotics. The company is also collaborating with semiconductor leaders to accelerate sensor and actuator integration through the NVIDIA Isaac Sim framework.

items.title: Microsoft Security Achieves 160x Improvement in AI Attack Detection with NVIDIA Partnership
items.summary: Microsoft Security reported significant advances in agentic AI safety through collaboration with NVIDIA on Nemotron and NemoClaw, achieving a 160x improvement in finding and mitigating AI-based attacks with real-time, adaptive runtime protection.

items.title: DGX Station Deployment Accelerates AI Innovation Across Multiple Sectors
items.summary: Industry leaders including Snowflake, EPRI, Medivis, and Sungkyunkwan University are deploying NVIDIA's DGX Station for applications ranging from AI-powered weather forecasting to protein structure analysis and surgical workflow integration.

items.title: Hong Kong Researchers Develop Explainable AI Architectures for Safer Systems
items.summary: Researchers in Hong Kong are advancing machine learning research focused on creating explainable artificial intelligence architectures using linguistic analysis and verification methods to improve AI system safety.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crystal Dynamics Announces Fourth Round of Layoffs in a Year
items.summary: Tomb Raider studio Crystal Dynamics has laid off an additional 20 staff members, marking the fourth round of layoffs within the past year.

items.title: Sony Shutting Down PlayStation VR2 Exclusive Firewall Ultra
items.summary: Sony is quietly ending support for Firewall Ultra, one of its two PSVR2 exclusives, with servers shutting down in September as revealed in an updated blog post.

items.title: Pearl Abyss Shares Plummet 30% After Poor Crimson Desert Reviews
items.summary: The share price of Crimson Desert developer Pearl Abyss dropped nearly 30% following disappointing early reviews of the visually stunning adventure game.

items.title: Red Storm Entertainment to Lay Off 105 Jobs and End Game Development
items.summary: Cary-based video game studio Red Storm is ceasing game development and laying off 105 employees at its headquarters.

items.title: Nvidia DLSS 5 Reveal Trailer Receives Heavy Backlash on YouTube
items.summary: The reveal trailer for Nvidia's DLSS 5 has only 16% likes on YouTube amid widespread player criticism, with similar poor reception for related tech demos.

items.title: FBC: Firebreak Receives Final Major Update, Price Drop, and Free Friend’s Pass
items.summary: Remedy Entertainment issued the last major update for FBC: Firebreak, including a price reduction and free Friend’s Pass, stating the game will remain playable for years.

items.title: Xbox March Update Brings New Features and Game Announcements
items.summary: Xbox's latest March update introduces substantial new features for insiders and announces games like a Subnautica sequel on day-one Game Pass, plus Starfield content updates.

items.title: Steam Spring Sale 2026 Launches with Major PC Game Discounts
items.summary: The Steam Spring Sale is live until March 26, featuring big deals on PC games as highlighted in recent coverage.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
